hypothesis: The whole accumulated inverse word admits an exact finite transducer description, but any successful global invariant must go beyond additive letter weights and the currently known short fringe language.
interpretation: The full growing middle is exactly a four-state right-to-left scan. Single-letter additive telescoping statistics are completely trivial, and the three known forbidden branch words alone permit ten consecutive zero pairs. The next proof attempt must couple a nonlinear or growing-memory transducer observable to the exact autonomous fringe orbit.
limitations: does not classify nonlinear or growing-memory cocycles
does not claim the finite branch counterexample occurs in the exact fringe orbit
does not prove the alternating survivor has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The four-state transducer identity and additive-cocycle classification are all-word statements proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_global_transducer.md. The analyzer exhausts all words through length seven as an implementation cross-check and verifies one finite branch-language counterexample.
question: problem1

additive_cocycle_no_go.interpretation: the only additive word statistic is a multiple of word length; the only state potential is constant

exact_conclusions.additive_no_go: no nontrivial single-letter additive cocycle telescopes the growing middle
exact_conclusions.global_reformulation: the complete growing word is scanned by a four-state right-to-left transducer and finite support means its terminal state is eventually 00
exact_conclusions.short_language_no_go: the forbidden words uu, ttttt, and ututtu are insufficient without the exact autonomous fringe orbit

short_language_counterexample.exact_conclusion: the three known forbidden branch words alone do not bound survivor zero-pair runs
short_language_counterexample.scope_warning: this finite branch word is not claimed to occur in the exact autonomous fringe orbit

whole_word_transducer.exact_identity: right-to-left scan returns (G|_v, G(v)) for every binary pair v
